words.def: a female parent of a child or animal; a person who is acting as a mother to a child
words.example: I want to buy a present for my mother and father.

words.def: a very high hill, often with rocks near the top
a small animal that is covered in fur and has a long thin tail. Mice live in fields, in people’s houses or where food is stored.
the opening in the face used for speaking, eating, etc.; the area inside the head behind this opening
words.example: She opened her mouth to say something.

words.def: a series of moving pictures recorded with sound that tells a story, watched at a movie theater or on a television or other device
to a great degree
words.example: Thank you very much for the flowers.

words.def: a mother
sounds that are arranged in a way that is pleasant or exciting to listen to. People sing music or play it on instruments.
words.example: I like any kind of pop or dance music.

words.def: used to say that something is necessary or very important (sometimes involving a rule or a law)
words.example: All visitors must report to reception.

words.def: of or belonging to the speaker or writer
a word or words that a particular person, animal, place or thing is known by
a person who lives next to you or near you
words.example: We've had a lot of support from all our friends and neighbours.

words.def: not at any time; not on any occasion
not existing before; recently made, invented, introduced, etc.
words.example: Have you read her new novel?

words.def: new information about something that has happened recently
a set of large printed sheets of paper, or a website, containing news, articles, advertisements, etc. and published every day or every week; the organization responsible for producing this
pleasant or attractive
the time between one day and the next when it is dark, when people usually sleep
words.example: at night, These animals only come out at night.
There are only nine of these rare animals left.
The temperature must be in the nineties today.

words.def: not one; not any; not a
words.example: No student is to leave the room.

words.def: the part of the face that sticks out above the mouth, used for breathing and smelling things
words.example: She had dark eyes and a long narrow nose.

words.def: used with be, do or have to form the negative of verbs; used to form the negative of modal verbs like can or must
not anything; no single thing
words.example: There was nothing in her bag.

words.def: the 11th month of the year, between October and December
a person whose job is to take care of sick or injured people, usually in a hospital
used with the numbers 1 to 12 when telling the time, to mean an exact hour
words.example: He left between five and six o'clock.

words.def: the 10th month of the year, between September and November
belonging to somebody; relating to somebody
used to say that something has been removed or become separated
words.example: He's had his beard shaved off.

words.def: a room, set of rooms or building where people work, usually sitting at desks
words.example: The company is moving to new offices on the other side of town.

words.def: many times
used when you are reacting to something that has been said, especially if you did not know it before
words.example: ‘I saw Ben yesterday.’ ‘Oh yes, how is he?’

words.def: safe and well; in a calm or happy state
of a particular age
words.example: be… years, months, etc. old, The baby was only a few hours old.

words.def: the number 1
words.example: Do you want one or two?

words.def: used to say that no other or others of the same group exist or are there
allowing things or people to go through; not closed or blocked
words.example: A wasp flew in the open window.

words.def: used to introduce another possibility
words.example: Is your sister older or younger than you?

words.def: between red and yellow in colour
the way in which people or things are placed or arranged in relation to each other
words.example: in… order, The names are listed in alphabetical order.

words.def: used to refer to people or things that are additional or different to people or things that have been mentioned or are known about
words.example: Mr Harris and Mrs Bate and three other teachers were there.

words.def: belonging to us; connected with us
away from the inside of a place or thing
words.example: She ran out into the corridor.

words.def: one side or both sides of a sheet of paper in a book, magazine, etc.
a liquid that is put on surfaces to give them a particular colour; a layer of this liquid when it has dried on a surface
a picture that has been painted
two things of the same type, especially when they are used or worn together
the thin material that you write and draw on and that is also used for wrapping and packing things
a section of a piece of writing, usually consisting of several sentences dealing with a single subject. The first sentence of a paragraph starts on a new line.
a person’s father or mother
words.example: He's still living with his parents.

words.def: an area of public land in a town or a city where people go to walk, play and relax
a person that you are doing an activity with, such as dancing or playing a game
a social occasion, often in a person’s home, at which people eat, drink, talk, dance and enjoy themselves
an instrument made of plastic or metal used for writing with ink (= coloured liquid for writing, etc.)
a narrow piece of wood, or a metal or plastic case, containing a black or coloured substance, used for drawing or writing
words.example: I'll get a pencil and paper.

words.def: human beings; men, women and children
words.example: At least ten people were killed in the crash.

words.def: a particular length of time
a human as an individual
words.example: What sort of person would do a thing like that?

words.def: your own; not belonging to or connected with anyone else
words.example: The novel is written from personal experience.

words.def: a piece of equipment for talking to people who are not in the same place as you
words.example: I have to make a phone call.

words.def: a picture that is made by using a camera that stores images in digital form or that has a film sensitive to light inside it
a large musical instrument played by pressing the black and white keys on the keyboard. The sound is produced by small hammers hitting the metal strings inside the piano.
an amount of something that has been cut or separated from the rest of it; a standard amount of something
words.example: piece of something, She wrote something on a small piece of paper.

words.def: an animal with pink, black or brown skin, short legs, a broad nose and a short curly tail. Pigs are kept on farms for their meat (called pork) or live in the wild.
pale red in colour
something that you intend to do or achieve
words.example: plan for something, Do you have any plans for the summer?

words.def: a flying vehicle with wings and one or more engines
a piece of writing performed by actors in a theatre or on television or radio
a person who takes part in a game or sport
an area of water that has been created for people to swim in
having very little money; not having enough money for basic needs
words.example: They were too poor to buy shoes for the kids.

words.def: the official system used for sending and delivering letters, packages, etc.
words.example: by post, I'll send the original to you by post.

words.def: a round white vegetable with a brown or red skin that grows underground as part of a plant also called a potato
words.example: Will you peel the potatoes for me?

words.def: doing an activity or training regularly so that you can improve your skill; the time you spend doing this
to do an activity or train regularly so that you can improve your skill
words.example: You need to practise every day.

words.def: attractive without being very beautiful
a thing that is difficult to deal with or to understand
something that people watch on television or listen to on the radio
to move something into a particular place or position
words.example: Put the cases down there, please.

words.def: one of four equal parts of something
words.example: quarter of something, a quarter of an hour/a century

words.def: fast
making very little noise
the activity of broadcasting programmes for people to listen to; the programmes that are broadcast
words.example: The play was written specially for radio.

words.def: water that falls from the sky in separate drops
words.example: There will be rain in all parts tomorrow.

words.def: to look at and understand the meaning of written or printed words or symbols
a person who reads, especially one who reads a lot or in a particular way
words.example: an avid reader of science fiction

words.def: the activity of somebody who reads
words.example: My hobbies include reading and painting.

words.def: fully prepared for what you are going to do and able to start it immediately
actually existing or happening and not imagined or pretended
used to emphasize an adjective or adverb
a cause or an explanation for something that has happened or that somebody has done
words.example: He said no but he didn't give a reason.

words.def: having the colour of blood or fire
to have or keep an image in your memory of an event, a person, a place, etc. from the past
words.example: remember somebody/something, This is Carla. Do you remember her?

words.def: a place where you can buy and eat a meal
words.example: We're going to try this Italian restaurant that just opened.

words.def: short, narrow white or brown grain grown on wet land in hot countries as food; the plant that produces this grain
having a lot of money or property
words.example: She's one of the richest women in the world.

words.def: true or correct as a fact
words.example: Did you get the answer right?

words.def: a natural flow of water that continues in a long line across land to the sea
words.example: Eventually we came to the mouth of the River Thames.

words.def: a part of a building that has its own walls, floor and ceiling and is usually used for a particular purpose
words.example: I heard him enter the room.

words.def: unhappy or showing unhappiness
words.example: sad to do something, We are very sad to hear that you are leaving.

words.def: a mixture of raw vegetables such as lettuce, tomato and cucumber, usually served with other food as part of a meal
words.example: All main courses come with salad or vegetables.

words.def: exactly the one or ones referred to or mentioned; not different
words.example: We have lived in the same house for twenty years.

words.def: two slices of bread, often spread with butter, with a layer of meat, cheese, etc. between them
the day of the week after Friday and before Sunday
